text: The surgeon performs laparoscopic esophagectomy and gastric pull-up, beginning with the mobilization of the stomach using ultrasonic dissection. The gastrocolic ligament is divided while preserving the gastroepiploic vessels, followed by the division of the short gastric vessels using an ultrasonic dissector.

The surgeon approaches the left gastric vessels from below the stomach, visualizing and dissecting the right and left cruciate. The abdominal portion of the esophagus is dissected free, and the left gastric vein is divided between absorbable clips.

The surgeon divides the left gastric artery between clips while preserving the accessory hepatic artery. A gastric tube is fashioned using multiple linear stapler applications, with the first shot through the right-sided cannula and subsequent shots through the left-sided cannula. The hiatus is widened, and the esophagus is dissected bluntly and with ultrasonic dissection up to the level of the esikos vein, following a plane along the anterior surface of the aorta. Care is taken to avoid opening the pleural sheath, and upward traction of mediastinal structures is performed cautiously to prevent cardiac rhythm alteration. The dissection of the upper esophagus is completed from the neck.

The surgeon performs the gastric pull-up by applying gentle traction on the esophagus from the neck incision, guided by laparoscopic visualization. The gastric tube is tailored with additional linear stapler applications. A soft silicone drainage is inserted through the hiatus to drain the mediastinum. The esophagogastric anastomosis is completed using the Orringer technique, with the axis opening closed by interrupted absorbable sutures. The bottom end of the gastric tube is wrapped around the posterior aspect of the proximal esophageal stump.
